prompt:
We're dropping the LaunchDarkly contract and I've been volunteered to write the in-house evaluation core. The JS and Python SDKs are already done, so the Go one has to bucket users EXACTLY the same way or a user gets the new checkout on web and the old one in the app: bucket = FNV-1a 64-bit hash of "<flagKey>:<unitID>" modulo 10000 basis points, exposed as Bucket(key, id string) int. A user is inside an N-bps rollout iff their bucket < N — that inequality is what makes ramping 30%->70% only ever add users, never kick anyone out.

Module go-flagengine, package flagengine, stdlib only. New() *Engine, then:
- AddEnv(name, parent string) error — environments form a tree (parent "" = root); adding under an unknown parent, an empty name, or a duplicate name are errors.
- SetFlag(env string, f Flag) error — upsert by flag key into that env. Flag{Key string; On bool; Rules []Rule; RolloutBPS int}. Validate: non-empty key, RolloutBPS in [0,10000], every clause well-formed. Rule{Clauses []Clause; Value bool}; Clause{Attr, Op string; Values []string} with ops "in" and "not_in" only.
- Eval(env, key string, u User) (Result, error) with User{ID string; Attrs map[string]string} and Result{Value bool; Reason string; Env string}. Lookup walks the env chain from the env upward and uses the NEAREST definition; Result.Env says which env supplied it. Missing flag anywhere on the chain -> ErrFlagNotFound (exported, errors.Is-able); unknown env -> error.

Evaluation order, and every decision must say why via Reason: flag off -> false with reason "off" (rules and rollout don't matter). Otherwise rules in order, first rule whose clauses ALL match serves its Value with reason "rule:<index>". "in" means the attribute is present and equals one of Values; "not_in" means present and equals none of them — an absent attribute satisfies NEITHER (support learned that one the hard way). No rule hit -> the percentage rollout: in-bucket serves true with reason "rollout", out-of-bucket false with reason "fallthrough". Handle nil Attrs.

SetFlag happens from the config-poller goroutine while request goroutines Eval, so the engine must be race-clean. engine_test.go and rollout_test.go are the acceptance contract — hands off; go test -race -timeout 30s ./... green. Structure it as a few focused files (env tree, rule matching, bucketing, engine), not one blob.
